name: What happened in that 2026 New York AI legal case I keep hearing about?
description: 2026 NY case *U.S. v. Heppner* ruled AI chats lack privilege and are discoverable.[1]

mainEntity.name: What happened in that 2026 New York AI legal case I keep hearing about?

acceptedAnswer.text: 2026 NY case *U.S. v. Heppner* ruled AI chats lack privilege and are discoverable.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: In *United States v. Heppner* (S.D.N.Y., Feb 2026), the court ruled defendant’s Claude AI chats were not privileged and discoverable.[1] The ruling emphasized AI fails attorney-client privilege elements: no licensed attorney, no confidentiality, not for formal advice.[1] Key factors: AI itself disclaimed legal role; chats used as evidence against defendant; narrow exception for lawyer-directed AI.[1] Review your own AI use history; discuss with counsel for case impact; prioritize human legal advice.[1] This sets precedent for civil cases too.
